titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Embassy in the United Kingdom

head.hi: The Secretary of State to the Embassy in the United Kingdom

div.p: 1480. Secy Defense upon advice Joint Chiefs of Staff apprvd in genIn a letter of April 23 to Secretary Acheson, not printed. proposed course action outlined ltr sent you instr 186, Apr 18.Not printed, but see footnote 1, p. 205. He suggested fol modification:

quote.p: “It is our opinion the Brit shld be informed forthwith that, irrespective of whether the talks at Fayid are, or are not, the source of continued embarrassment, any further talks between members of Admiral Conolly’s staff and Brit shld be held at a point outside of Egypt, such as Malta, Cyprus or Cyrenaica.”

div.p: Validity comments urtel 1636 Apr 28 appreciated but Dept believes that in view of circumstances Joint Chiefs of Staff opinion in this matter shld be binding on Dept and modification has been accepted.In a letter of April 29 from Acting Secretary of State Webb to the Secretary of Defense, not printed. (883.20/5–249) Brit Emb being informed.On May 2 (memorandum by Mr. Mattison of his conversation with Thomas E. Bromley, First Secretary of the British Embassy, 883.20/5–249). Urtel 1636 being transmitted NME.The editors have been unable to find any documentation in the files of the Department of State which reflect the actual exchange of views by the staff of Admiral Conolly with British military officials.
